title: agent.session.linked v1
description: Declares a sibling/peer/subordinate/council relationship between two existing sessions. Emitted by a freshly-spawned session's SessionStart hook when AGENT_PARENT_SESSION_ID is set, OR by 'nervous session link' for manual post-hoc linking. Carries the human-initiated guarantee via 'spawned_by': the chain MUST terminate at null (a human invocation). Schema validates the shape; publisher-layer validation enforces the chain-terminates-at-null property by walking ancestors.

pane_id_qualified.description: Primary durable key for the sibling (newly-linked) session: 'zellij:<session_name>:<pane_id>' or 'tmux:<pane_id>'. Optional only because non-pane CLIs may lack it.

session_id.description: Secondary key for the sibling session. Alias of sibling_session_id, included for consistency with the other agent.session.* schemas.

main_session_id.description: session_id of the 'main' anchor session this link attaches to.

sibling_session_id.description: session_id of the newly-linked session (the one emitting this event).

mode.description: Collaboration mode. 1:1 mapping to role, kept as a separate field so consumers don't have to translate.

linked_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp at which the link was established.

spawned_by.description: Human-initiated guarantee: null indicates a human-invoked spawn (the chain terminator); a session_id indicates another session helped spawn this one. The full ancestor chain MUST terminate at null — publishers walk ancestors and reject loops or daemon-spawned chains. Schema permits both shapes; chain-termination is enforced at the publisher layer.

epic_id.description: Optional epic correlation id (see agent.epic.<id>.spec.v1). Set when this link is part of a multi-sibling epic.

project_pair.description: Optional cross-project hint, e.g. 'nervous-bus<->tengine'. Convention only; consumers MAY parse but SHOULD NOT rely on the format.
